source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/TimeSeries/ARAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D6 STRUCTURAL oracle (D5 seeded-scalar assertions removed; see below). ARAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over an AR(1) AutoRegressive model, then builds the posterior forecast ensemble (mode/mean/credible band) over the observed range plus forecasting_time_steps. training_time_steps is pinned to 15 (the model default of max(30, floor(0.8*n)) exceeds this 20-point series and would be invalid). curve_length = 20 observed + 3 forecast = 23; mode_curve[0] is the deterministic first observed value. Both reproduce exactly across C++/R/Python AND the real-RMC.BestFit emitter (tools/oracle_emitter, BuildAndRunAnalysis mirroring ar_analysis::run(): sim-defaults guard, serial seeded fit, CreateUncertaintyAnalysisResults). D6 REALITY: the emitter does NOT reproduce the D5 self-computed seeded scalars (point-estimate parameter, AIC/BIC/DIC/RMSE) -- the ported C++ AR analysis and the real C# ARAnalysis land on qualitatively different posterior modes for this short chaotic chain (C++ MAP intercept ~58 / DIC ~-960 vs real C# ~16 / DIC ~46). This matches the D6 brief's design statement that the time-series analyses are STRUCTURAL oracles only -- no numeric MCMC oracle exists upstream. The seeded scalars were therefore removed rather than pinned to an un-cross-reproducible C++-only value; only the deterministic structural invariants are asserted. thinning_interval pinned to 1; short run (iterations 100, output_length 400).
